section.heading: CLEEN engine and airframe technology partnership

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content: The Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration shall enter into a cost-sharing cooperative agreement, using a competitive process, with institutions, entities, or consortiums to carry out a program for the development, maturation, and testing of certifiable CLEEN aircraft, engine technologies, and jet fuels for civil airplanes.

subsection.heading: CLEEN Engine and Airframe Technology Defined.—
subsection.content: In this section, the term “CLEEN aircraft and engine technology” means continuous lower energy, emissions, and noise aircraft and engine technology.

subsection.heading: Performance Objective.—
subsection.content: The Administrator shall establish the performance objectives for the program in terms of the specific objectives to reduce fuel burn, emissions and noise.

subsection.heading: Selection.—
subsection.content: In carrying out the program, the Administrator may provide that not less than 2 of the cooperative agreements entered into under this section involve the participation of an entity that is a small business concern (as defined in section 3 of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 632)), provided that the submitted technology proposal of the entity meets, at a minimum, FAA Acquisition Management System requirements and requisite technology readiness levels for entry into the agreement, as determined by the Administrator.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 115–254, div. B, title VII, § 743(a), Oct. 5, 2018, 132 Stat. 3413; amended Pub. L. 118–63, title X, § 1008, May 16, 2024, 138 Stat. 1389.)

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2024—Subsec. (a). Pub. L. 118–63, § 1008(1), struck out “subsonic” after “fuels for civil”.
